title: vignea paniculata subsp. lusitanica
intro: Vignea paniculata subsp. lusitanica, also known as the Butcher's Broom or Ruscus aculeatus, is a flowering plant native to Europe and Asia, and is commonly used as a medicinal herb. It has been traditionally used to treat varicose veins, hemorrhoids, and other circulatory problems due to its anti-inflammatory and astringent properties. The health benefits of Vignea paniculata subsp. lusitanica include improved blood circulation, reduced inflammation, and relief from swelling and pain. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, tinctures, and dried roots, which can be used internally or applied topically to alleviate symptoms.
